name: Less
comment:
Makes the `Url` the single source of truth for the state of your user interface,
and hides the corresponding messages from your `update`.

@docs application, Application, mapDocument, reroute, Document

---


## Why?

In the conventional Elm architecture, if you want the `view` to respect state encoded in the `Url` such as queries or flags, you need your `update` to incorporate this state into the in-memory application Model, which is eventually interpreted in the `view`:

     - - Url ‚Üė               Cmd ‚≠Ę Url' - - -
             Message ‚Üė      ‚Üó
                      update
     - - - - - Model ‚Üó      ‚Üė Model' - - - - -
                                  ‚Üė
                                  view

This opens two possible pitfalls:

  - The Url can potentially manipulate parts of the Model that are not only intended for viewing, for example data shared with the backend
  - The Model needs to parse the `Url` and pass it to the `view`, potentially losing or misinterpreting information.


## How?

    - - - - - - Url ‚Üė              ‚Üó Url' - -
                 Path, Flags, Fragment
                               ‚Üė
                              view
             Message ‚Üė         ‚Üó
    - - - - -  Model ‚Üė    ‚Üó Model' - - - - -
                     update
                          ‚Üė Cmd

aliases.name: Document
aliases.comment:
Parametrizes [Browser.Document](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/elm/browser/latest/Browser#Document)
over the current and previous [`State`](Less-Link#State).
aliases.type: { current : Less.Link.State, previous : Maybe.Maybe Less.Link.State } -> Browser.Document (Less.Link.Msg msg)

values.name: application
values.comment: Keeps the Ui state in the Url.
values.type: { init : ( model, Platform.Cmd.Cmd modelMsg ), update : modelMsg -> model -> Return.Return modelMsg model, view : model -> Less.Document modelMsg } -> Less.Application model modelMsg

values.name: mapDocument
values.comment: Render the document and map it to `Html`.
values.type: (html -> List.List (Html.Html (Less.Link.Msg msg))) -> { body : Less.Ui.Ui region html wrapper, layout : Less.Ui.Layout region narrowHtml_ html narrowWrapper_ wrapper, title : String.String } -> Less.Document msg

values.name: reroute
values.comment:
If you want to re-route Urls before rendering, compose such functions left of the Document:

    import Less.Link

    myDocument : Document msg
    myDocument =
        { body = ..., layout = ..., title = "..." }
            |> mapDocument identity

    myReroute : Less.State -> Less.State
    myReroute =
        Less.Link.mapLocation
            (\location -> if location == "/" then "/newHomePage" else location)

    myView : () -> Document msg
    myView () =
        Less.reroute myReroute >> myDocument
values.type: (Less.Link.State -> Less.Link.State) -> Less.Document msg -> { current : Less.Link.State, previous : Maybe.Maybe Less.Link.State } -> Browser.Document (Less.Link.Msg msg)

name: Less.Link

unions.name: Link
unions.comment:
Relative changes to the Url are only applied when a link opens in the same tab.
Otherwise, absolute changes are applied by the Elm runtime.

**Example:**

The user clicks a menu (implemented as a `Toggle`) to open it, then clicks again to close it.

(a)
They decide to share the menu, so they right-click on the toggle and choose 'copy link'.
Their friend opens the link and the handle is activated. Otherwise, the friend's Ui is at the default
state. A toggle's `href` always points to the `open` state.

(b)
They copy the Url and paste it in another tab or browser or device.
The app loads and restores exactly the same Ui state as in the original tab.

  - Jump Navigation
      - [x] ¬†[With a single target (goTo)](Less-Ui-Html#goTo)
      - [x] ¬†[With two back-and-forth targets (bounce)](Less-Ui-Html#bounce)

  - Progressive Disclosure
      - [x] Orthogonal; any number of [Flags](#Flag) can [be activated (toggle)](Less-Ui-Html#toggle)
        - [x][+ React to flags set somewhere else (atFlag)](Less-Ui-Html#atFlag)
      - [ ] Exactly one active at a given Ui node (tab) ‚ėě [#8](https://github.com/upsiflu/restrictive/issues/8) ‚ėě [#2](https://github.com/upsiflu/restrictive/issues/2)
      - [ ] One or zero active in the browser tab (dropdown, dialog, popover) ‚ėě [#8](https://github.com/upsiflu/restrictive/issues/8)

  - Filter
      - [x][Search](Less-Ui-Html#search)
        - [x][+ React to filters set somewhere else](Less-Ui-Html#filter) a [Category](#Category)

unions.name: Msg
unions.comment: Handles your message type (`modelMsg`) as well as changes to the Ui state (Url).

unions.name: Mutation
unions.comment: Marks the delta from the previous to the current state. A String can describe the Aria-"set" (such as "page") that the link refers to.

aliases.name: Category
aliases.comment: Distinguish parallel search inputs on a screen.

aliases.name: Flag
aliases.comment:
**Progressive disclosure**: Turning off a `Flag` renders all corresponding bits
of `Ui` invisible.
Example: [Ui.Html#toggle](Less-Ui-Html#toggle).

**Parametric search:** Assignments such as `?a=b` may represent currently active Tabs
or a search string.
Example: [Ui.Html#search](Less-Ui-Html#search).

aliases.name: SearchTerm
aliases.comment: Represent a filter value or the search input.

aliases.name: State
aliases.comment:
- path : String
  - query : Maybe String
  - fragment : Maybe String

values.name: apply
values.comment: Think `update` in TEA.
values.type: Less.Link.Link -> Less.Link.State -> ( { pushHistoryState : Basics.Bool }, Less.Link.State )

values.name: fromUrl
values.comment:
This happens when a Link is clicked. We want to apply the relative change here.

TODO: What if several links are encoded in the Url?
values.type: Url.Url -> Less.Link.Link

values.name: getSpaceSeparatedSearchTerms
values.comment: Space-separate, then intersect, all assignments to `category`.
values.type: Less.Link.Category -> Url.Url -> List.List Less.Link.SearchTerm

values.name: getStateSearchTerms
values.comment:
Note that a `Category` may contain "=":

    import Url

    Url.fromString "http://test/?a=b=c"
        |> Maybe.map (getStateSearchTerms "a")
        --> Just ["b=c"]

    Url.fromString "http://test/?a=b=c"
        |> Maybe.map (getStateSearchTerms "a=")
        --> Just []

    Url.fromString "http://test/?a=b=c"
        |> Maybe.map (getStateSearchTerms "a=b")
        --> Just ["c"]

Attention: Categories "toggle" and "bounce" are interpreted as redirect directives in relative links ([-> Link](#Link)).
values.type: Less.Link.Category -> Url.Url -> List.List Less.Link.SearchTerm

values.name: mapLocation
values.comment:
Re-route the Url before the Ui is rendered.

**Use cases:**

  - You want to fuzzy-match locations (typos)
  - You want to accept aliases for certain paths or fragments
  - You have deleted a page and want to catch and redirect it to another page
values.type: (Less.Link.Location -> Less.Link.Location) -> Less.Link.State -> Less.Link.State

values.name: mutationFromTwoStates
values.comment: Probe the delta between two states against a [Link](#Link) and, with an optional Aria set name such as "page", generate a [Mutation](#Mutation).
values.type: { current : Less.Link.State, previous : Maybe.Maybe Less.Link.State } -> Less.Link.Link -> Maybe.Maybe String.String -> Less.Link.Mutation

values.name: parseLocation
values.comment:
parseLocation ""
        -> OnlyPath ""

    parseLocation "/"
        -> OnlyPath ""

    parseLocation "#"
        -> OnlyFragment ""

    parseLocation "/#"
        -> PathAndFragment "" ""
values.type: Less.Link.Location -> Less.Link.ParsedLocation

values.name: sanitize
values.comment: Internal function to remove magical assignments ("toggle" and "bounce").
values.type: Url.Url -> Less.Link.State

values.name: toHref
values.comment: Use with `Html.Attributes.href`. Will not include the current state. `Toggle` and `Bounce` will produce an extra flag enabling switchy changes.
values.type: Less.Link.Link -> String.String

name: Less.Ui
comment:
Separate [State](Less-Link#State) and [Layout](#Layout) of interface elements from the main model
and build accessible patterns orthogonal to the Dom tree.

@docs Ui, Item


# Create

@docs singleton, wrap


# Modify

@docs inRegion


# Append

**[`a ++ b`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/elm/core/latest/Basics#++)**


# Apply state

@docs view

@docs Layout, CurrentLayout, Wrapper, applyStates

---


# Convenience

@docs repeat

In addition, many List functions directly work with `Ui`, for example `List.length`, `List.reverse` or `List.Extra.permutations`.
Caveats are discussed in [Advanced Usage](#advanced-usage)


### Decompose

@docs uncons


# Working with Regions

@docs OrHeader

---


# Advanced Usage

@docs map

Since `Ui`s are `List`s, it is easy to use the library functions from the `List` and `List.Extra` packages.
However, I recommend against it.

    {-| Combines items from two `Ui`s. If one `Ui` is longer, its excessive elements are dropped.
    -}
    map2 fu a b =
        List.map2 fu (List.map List.singleton a) (List.map List.singleton b)
            |> List.concat

    map3 fu a b c =
        List.map3 fu
            (List.map List.singleton a)
            (List.map List.singleton b)
            (List.map List.singleton c)
            |> List.concat

The big drawback when using `Ui`s as `List`s is that you cannot inspect (compare, filter, sort) them because
the `Item` type is opaque.
It is usually easier to build exactly the `Ui` you need instead of altering and recombining them after the fact.

@docs indexedMapList
@docs mapEach

@docs mutateWrappers


# Slated for removal

The following exports have no application and may be removed in the next release.

(currently nothing)

unions.name: OrHeader
unions.comment: Extend a sum type by a `Header` constructor. Every app in `Less` has a Header.

unions.name: Wrapper
unions.comment:
Create a custom `wrapper` to sneak functions into a
functionless `Ui`. At the `layout` phase, you decide what
your `wrapper` does:

  - `Wrapped`: Transform the rendered `html` inside the current region.
  - `Keyed`: Like Html.Keyed
  - `Nested`: Render a `Ui` with a different `html` type and then
    nest it in a supplied function.
    This transformation is applied parallelly within each region.
  - `Stateful`: Render a link or filter. The Url determines whether
    the enclosed Ui is rendered or not. You have to apply the state early.

You can either use the provided `Wrapper` or roll your own.
Advantage of rolling your own `wrapper` type: You don't need to store functions in the Ui,
which makes it comparable and serializable.

See [`Ui.Html`](Less-Ui-Html) for an example of a mostly defunctionalized wrapper.
cases: { howToWrapCurrentRegion : html -> html, howToWrapOtherRegions : html -> html }
List.List ( String.String, Less.Ui.Ui region html wrapper )

{ regions : List.List region, narrowLayout : Less.Ui.CurrentLayout region narrowHtml narrowHtml narrowWrapper narrowWrapper, combine : { makeInnerHtml : Less.Ui.Ui region narrowHtml narrowWrapper -> Maybe.Maybe narrowHtml } -> html }

aliases.name: CurrentLayout
aliases.comment:
This type of Layout has its current and previous state already applied
through [applyStates](#applyStates).
aliases.type: { wrap : customWrapper -> Less.Ui.Wrapper region narrowHtml html narrowWrapper customWrapper, concat : List.List html -> html, concatDicts : List.List (AssocList.Dict (Less.Ui.OrHeader region) html) -> AssocList.Dict (Less.Ui.OrHeader region) html, arrange : { header : html, region : region -> html } -> html }

aliases.name: Layout
aliases.comment: The layout is a rule for mapping a Ui to an `html` tree.
aliases.type: { wrap : { current : Less.Link.State, previous : Maybe.Maybe Less.Link.State } -> customWrapper -> Less.Ui.Wrapper region narrowHtml html narrowWrapper customWrapper, concat : List.List html -> html, arrange : { header : html, region : region -> html } -> html }

aliases.name: Ui
aliases.comment:
none : Ui region html wrapper
    none =
        []

values.name: applyStates
values.comment: fix a Layout to the current states to make it ["current"](#CurrentLayout).
values.type: { current : Less.Link.State, previous : Maybe.Maybe Less.Link.State } -> Less.Ui.Layout region narrowHtml html narrowWrapper customWrapper -> Less.Ui.CurrentLayout region narrowHtml html narrowWrapper customWrapper

values.name: inRegion
values.comment:
Designate a region for each descendant.

Note that the last designation "wins".

[#26: Once designated, `Ui`s can't be redesignated](https://github.com/upsiflu/restrictive/issues/26)
values.type: region -> Less.Ui.Ui region html wrapper -> Less.Ui.Ui region html wrapper

values.name: indexedMapList
values.comment:
Modify items `Ui`s according to their order. For example, zip their indices between the elements:

    singleton [1008] ++ singleton [2004] ++ singleton [1007]
        |> indexedMapList (\i -> (++) (singleton [i]))
        |> List.length
            --> 6
values.type: (Basics.Int -> Less.Ui.Ui region html wrapper -> Less.Ui.Ui region html wrapper) -> Less.Ui.Ui region html wrapper -> Less.Ui.Ui region html wrapper

values.name: map
values.comment:
Modify the type of `html` in the Ui.

‚ö†ÔłŹ If you can, build up your Ui with the final `html` type.

-- Todo: Tail Call Optimize.
values.type: (html -> html2) -> Less.Ui.Ui region html wrapper -> Less.Ui.Ui region html2 wrapper

values.name: mapEach
values.comment:
Modify each descendent as a separate Ui and then recombine them.

    region html attribute wrapper   "A" ++ region html attribute wrapper   "B" ++ region html attribute wrapper   "C"
        |> mapEach ((++) (html ", "))
        ---> something like A, B, C
values.type: (Less.Ui.Ui region html wrapper -> Less.Ui.Ui region2 html2 wrapper2) -> Less.Ui.Ui region html wrapper -> Less.Ui.Ui region2 html2 wrapper2

values.name: mutateWrappers
values.comment:
Modify the type of `wrapper` in the Ui and make sure every direct descendant is a wrapper so it can be modified

‚ö†ÔłŹ If you can, build up your Ui with the final `wrapper` type.
values.type: { leafToWrapper : html -> wrapper } -> (wrapper -> wrapper2) -> Less.Ui.Ui region html wrapper -> Less.Ui.Ui region html wrapper2

values.name: repeat
values.comment:
repeat n =
        List.repeat n >> List.concat
values.type: Basics.Int -> Less.Ui.Ui region html wrapper -> Less.Ui.Ui region html wrapper

values.name: singleton
values.comment: ūüźĆ
values.type: html -> Less.Ui.Ui region_ html wrapper_

values.name: uncons
values.comment: Attempt to separate the first descendant in the Ui.
values.type: Less.Ui.Ui region html wrapper -> Maybe.Maybe ( Less.Ui.Ui region html wrapper, Less.Ui.Ui region html wrapper )

values.name: view
values.comment:
‚ö†ÔłŹ If you can, prefer one of the functions in [Less](Less) such as
[`application`](Less#application).
values.type: Less.Ui.CurrentLayout region narrowHtml_ html narrowWrapper_ wrapper -> Less.Ui.Ui region html wrapper -> html

values.name: wrap
values.comment: Check out [the default wrappers in `Less.Ui.Html`](Less-Ui-Html#wrap-the-dom).
values.type: wrapper -> Less.Ui.Ui region_ html_ wrapper

name: Less.Ui.Html

unions.name: Region
unions.comment:
**Scene:** the Item's editable contents and overlays

**Control:** Toolbar, Property sheet

**Info:** Status bar, Help screen, Tooltip bubble, Snack bar

aliases.name: Html
aliases.type: Less.Ui.Ui region (Less.Ui.Html.HtmlList (Less.Link.Msg msg)) (Less.Ui.Html.Wrapper region narrowMsg msg)

values.name: animations
values.comment:
Add delightful css transitions to reflect the Url transitions.

Works best when little additional css is added to your app.
values.type: Less.Ui.Html.Html region_ msg_ narrowMsg_

values.name: arrangeOverDefaultRegions
values.comment:
Lays out a traditional application layout with a sticky `Header`,
    scrolling `Scene`, and both `Control` and `Info` fixed to the bottom.
values.type: { header : Maybe.Maybe (Less.Ui.Html.HtmlList msg), region : Less.Ui.Html.Region -> Maybe.Maybe (Less.Ui.Html.HtmlList msg) } -> Less.Ui.Html.HtmlList msg

values.name: article
values.comment:
From w3.org:

> The article element represents a self-contained composition in a document, page, application, or site and that is, in principle, independently distributable or reusable, e.g. in syndication.
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> Less.Ui.Html.Html region narrowMsg msg -> Less.Ui.Html.Html region narrowMsg msg

values.name: atFlag
values.comment:
Show a `Ui` exactly when a certain `Flag` is present in the `Url`.
   Use [`toggle`](#toggle) to let the user toggle the flag.
values.type: Less.Link.Flag -> Less.Ui.Html.Html region narrowMsg msg -> Less.Ui.Html.Html region narrowMsg msg

values.name: bounce
values.comment:
Navigate to `there`, and from there, back `here`.
Will navigate `there` when the link is opened in a new tab or shared.
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute Basics.Never) -> { there : Less.Link.Location, here : Less.Link.Location, label : Less.Ui.Html.HtmlList msg } -> Less.Ui.Html.Html region narrowMsg msg -> Less.Ui.Html.Html region narrowMsg msg

values.name: disclose
values.comment:
From w3.org:

> The details element represents a disclosure widget from which the user can obtain additional information or controls.

> The summary element child of the element, if any, represents the summary or legend of the details.

Notes:

  - You can auto-open it with `Attr.attribute "open" ""`
  - It will also auto-open if you search the page and a match is inside
  - It is impossible to animate
  - If you want animations and reproducibility, use `toggle` instead
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> { summary : Less.Ui.Html.Html region narrowMsg msg, summaryAttrs : List.List (Html.Attribute msg) } -> Less.Ui.Html.Html region narrowMsg msg -> Less.Ui.Html.Html region narrowMsg msg

values.name: filter
values.comment:
Show a `Ui` according to what searchTerms are currently associated with a given category.

    -- Show a token for each SearchTerm in category "search=a"
    List.concatMap viewToken
        |> filter "search"
values.type: Less.Link.Category -> (List.List Less.Link.SearchTerm -> Less.Ui.Html.Html region narrowMsg msg) -> Less.Ui.Html.Html region narrowMsg msg

values.name: goTo
values.comment: Navigate to a `destination` and show the nested `Ui` when it's reached.
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute Basics.Never) -> { destination : Less.Link.Location, inHeader : Basics.Bool, label : Less.Ui.Html.HtmlList msg } -> Less.Ui.Html.Html region narrowMsg msg -> Less.Ui.Html.Html region narrowMsg msg

values.name: html
values.comment: ūüźĆ
values.type: Less.Ui.Html.HtmlList msg -> Less.Ui.Html.Html region_ narrowMsg_ msg

values.name: keyedNode
values.comment:
Key a node with `Tuple.pair "uniqueKey"` such that the vDom diffing algorithm preserves it even when it changes position in the list.

Note [ul](#ul) and [ol](#ol).

The functionality is taken directly from the standard library `Html.Keyed`.
values.type: String.String -> List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> List.List ( String.String, Less.Ui.Html.Html region narrowMsg msg ) -> Less.Ui.Html.Html region narrowMsg msg

values.name: layout
values.type: Less.Ui.Layout region (Less.Ui.Html.HtmlList (Less.Link.Msg narrowMsg)) (Less.Ui.Html.HtmlList (Less.Link.Msg msg)) (Less.Ui.Html.Wrapper region narrowMsg narrowMsg) (Less.Ui.Html.Wrapper region narrowMsg msg)

values.name: nest
values.comment:
Gives your Html widgets access to state information.

For example, if you want to extend a widget or form generator (`elm-any-type-forms`) that can only output Html
with `Ui` elements that alter and respond to the Url, then you need

  - a way to convert from `Ui (Msg narrowMsg)` to `Html (Msg narrowMsg)` nested inside the widget -> `makeInnerHtml`
  - a way to convert from the widget `Html (Msg msg)` to `Ui (Msg msg)` -> `combine`
  - a way to forward the current state to the nested `Ui`

Here is how you use this function:

1.  Write the `Ui` code for your widget extension.
    You can use all the local parameters your widget provides.

2.  Convert it to `Html (Msg narrowMsg)` using `makeInnerHtml`
    to make it fit inside your widget.

3.  Your widget will create `Html (Msg msg)`. The function you
    wrote for drawing the widget is the `combine` function that
    you can now `nest`.

**Caution:** If you use this functionality, the `Ui` will contain functions and will no longer support equality checks and serialisation.

**Note:** As of now, the type setup will limit you to **nest one layer deep**, i.e. the compiler will complain if you try to nest Ui in a widget that is part of the Ui nested in another widget.
This is because the inside of the widget uses a different message type than the outside of the widget. As of now, I see no other way to mitigate than to use code generation
in order to write out the `narrower` nested types. I've never done code generation, and I don't feel comfortable with long types, so I'll leave it to you.
values.type: { regions : List.List region, combine : { makeInnerHtml : Less.Ui.Html.NarrowUi region narrowMsg -> Maybe.Maybe (Less.Ui.Html.HtmlList (Less.Link.Msg narrowMsg)) } -> Less.Ui.Html.HtmlList (Less.Link.Msg msg) } -> Less.Ui.Html.Html region narrowMsg msg

values.name: node
values.comment:
Wrap all parts of the Ui that are in the current region in an arbitrary Html node.

    node "div" [] myUi
values.type: String.String -> List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> Less.Ui.Html.Html region narrowMsg msg -> Less.Ui.Html.Html region narrowMsg msg

values.name: ol
values.comment: Ordered List
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> List.List ( String.String, Less.Ui.Html.Html region narrowMsg msg ) -> Less.Ui.Html.Html region narrowMsg msg

values.name: search
values.comment: Display a search box and show a `Ui` according to what searchTerms are currently associated with a given category.
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute Basics.Never) -> { category : Less.Link.Category, inHeader : Basics.Bool, label : Less.Ui.Html.HtmlList msg } -> (List.List Less.Link.SearchTerm -> Less.Ui.Html.Html region narrowMsg msg) -> Less.Ui.Html.Html region narrowMsg msg

values.name: section
values.comment:
From w3.org:

> The section element represents a generic section of a document or application. A section, in this context, is a thematic grouping of content, typically with a heading.
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> Less.Ui.Html.Html region narrowMsg msg -> Less.Ui.Html.Html region narrowMsg msg

values.name: toggle
values.comment:
Toggle a `Flag` and show/hide the associated `Ui` accordingly.
Will add the flag when the link is opened in a new tab or shared, no matter its state in the current tab.

`Flag` is a string and may contain "=".
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute Basics.Never) -> { flag : Less.Link.Flag, inHeader : Basics.Bool, label : Less.Ui.Html.HtmlList msg } -> Less.Ui.Html.Html region narrowMsg msg -> Less.Ui.Html.Html region narrowMsg msg

values.name: ul
values.comment: Unordered List
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> List.List ( String.String, Less.Ui.Html.Html region narrowMsg msg ) -> Less.Ui.Html.Html region narrowMsg msg
